commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the remaining action is maintainer approval of the durable sender-metadata privacy boundary, not an automated code repair.; Needs attention: Needs maintainer attention because the diff intentionally persists senderId, senderName, and senderUsername in durable session JSONL; no dependency, CI, permission, or code-execution supply-chain regres... (sha=2369b60a763aecd26af1806ec4564a64601dc0c2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads SenderId, SenderName, and SenderUsername from reply session context into UserTurnInput, persists them under a __openclaw sender envelope in user-turn transcript JSONL, adds regression tests, and includes a small exec-policy type cleanup.

PR surface: Source +27, Tests +60. Total +87 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main carries sender identity in the inbound context, but the userTurnInput and persisted user-turn writer omit SenderId, SenderName, and SenderUsername. I did not run a live Telegram group message in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the remaining action is maintainer approval of the durable sender-metadata privacy boundary, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(sessions): persist sender metadata in user turn transcript JSONL
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
- Group chat user messages persisted to the session JSONL transcript were missing `__openclaw` sender identity metadata (`senderId`, `senderName`, `senderUsername`) that direct (1:1) session records already carry.
- Fix: Thread sender identity from the channel inbound context through `UserTurnInput` → `buildUserTurnSenderMeta` into the persisted JSONL `__openclaw` envelope. When no sender metadata is available, the `__openclaw` block is omitted.
- Scope: Session transcript persistence only. Does not change live turn handling, channel transport, or gateway behavior.

## Changes
- `src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.types.ts`: add `senderId`, `senderName`, `senderUsername` to `UserTurnInput`
- `src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.ts`: add `buildUserTurnSenderMeta()` and thread `__openclaw` into persisted message
- `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.ts`: pass `SenderId`/`SenderName`/`SenderUsername` from session context
- `src/infra/exec-policy.ts`: restore `...baseWithoutMode` spread so mode is not leaked when only security/ask fields are overridden
- `src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.test.ts`: two new regression tests (sender metadata, empty sender)

## Evidence

**Before (negative control — `git checkout origin/main -- src/sessions/ src/auto-reply/`):**

```
$ node --import tsx -e "..."
Has __openclaw: false
__openclaw: null
```

**After (fix applied):**

```
$ node --import tsx -e "..."
Has __openclaw: true
__openclaw: {"senderId":"8489979671","senderName":"Ram Shenoy","senderUsername":"ram_s"}
No-sender has __openclaw: false
```

**Vitest:**

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.test.ts --run
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  29 passed (29)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-policy.test.ts --run
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  2 passed (2)
```
target.author: pick-cat

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
